steps.title: Parse the equation structure
steps.content: The equation is $0.4t + 2 = 6$, where $t$ is time in months. The number 2 is a starting height (2 inches), and 6 is the target height (6 inches). The term $0.4t$ represents total growth over $t$ months.

steps.title: Identify the role of 0.4
steps.content: In a linear equation of the form $\text{rate} \times \text{time} + \text{start} = \text{end}$, the coefficient of $t$ is the rate. Here $0.4$ multiplies $t$ (months), so it represents the amount, in inches, that Maya's plant grows each month.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: The time, in months, it will take Maya's plant to grow 1 inch — 0.4 is a coefficient of $t$, not itself a time value. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: The height, in inches, Maya's plant will grow each month — matches the rate interpretation of the coefficient. Kept.
  • Choice C: The time, in months, it will take Maya's plant to reach a height of 6 inches — that is what $t$ represents, not 0.4. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: The height, in inches, Maya's plant will grow in 6 months — growth in 6 months would be $0.4 \times 6 = 2.4$, not 0.4. Eliminated.
That confirms B is correct.
